The Central Bank of India (CBI) has released an official recruitment notification for the engagement of 4500 Apprentice Posts under the Apprentices Act, 1961. This recruitment drive provides an excellent opportunity for graduates who want to gain practical experience in the banking sector and build a strong foundation for their future careers.
The apprenticeship program will be conducted across various states of India, offering selected candidates hands-on training in banking operations, customer service, financial products, and administrative functions. Interested candidates can submit their online application forms before 22 June 2026 through the official website.
Central Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026
Applicants are advised to carefully review the eligibility criteria, age limit, application fee, stipend details, selection process, and important dates before applying.
Central Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview
The Central Bank of India is one of India’s oldest and most trusted public sector banks. Established in 1911, the bank has a vast network of branches across the country and serves millions of customers.
The apprenticeship program is designed to provide fresh graduates with exposure to modern banking operations and practical workplace experience. Candidates selected under this recruitment drive will work in various branches and departments of the bank and gain valuable professional skills.

The Government of Haryana has announced a major initiative to improve the management and infrastructure of government hospitals across the state. Under this new plan, several non-medical services such as cleanliness, housekeeping, sanitation, gardening, and security management are expected to be operated through the Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model.
This decision is likely to create a large number of employment opportunities for eligible candidates across Haryana. According to available information, more than 1000 contractual vacancies may be generated for various support staff positions including Security Guards, Sweepers, Peons, Watchmen, Mali (Gardeners), Ward Attendants, Security Supervisors, and Sanitation Supervisors.
Haryana Govt Hospital Vacancy 2026
Many job seekers are expecting these vacancies to be filled through the Haryana Kaushal Rozgar Nigam (HKRN) portal, which has become one of the primary platforms for contractual recruitment in Haryana government departments.
Candidates who are looking for government-related contractual jobs in Haryana should stay updated and keep their HKRN profiles active. In this article, we provide complete information regarding eligibility, expected vacancies, age limit, salary, selection process, documents required, and the application procedure.
Haryana Govt Hospital Vacancy 2026 Overview
Haryana Govt Hospital Vacancy 2026 is expected to offer 1000+ contractual vacancies in government hospitals across Haryana. The recruitment may include posts such as Security Guard, Peon, Sweeper, Watchman, Mali, Ward Attendant, and Sanitation Supervisor. Candidates with qualifications ranging from 8th Pass to 12th Pass may be eligible to apply.

selectionSteps.title: The Selection Journey for NBA Delhi Office Assistant 2026
bullets: The National Board of Accreditation’s (NBA) selection procedure is intended to be strict. Since BECIL is in charge of hiring, they are searching for applicants who are “job-ready” from the start rather than merely qualified on paper.
What to anticipate at each stage and how to get through it are as follows:

selectionSteps.title: Step 1: First Shortlisting (Filter)
bullets: What happens: Officials will determine whether you fulfill the requirements for two years of experience and a 50% graduation mark.

The Human Touch: Use action verbs in the application instead of only listing your responsibilities. Instead of stating “worked as an assistant,” say, “managed office correspondence and digital records for 2 years.” During the manual shortlisting process, this makes your profile stand out.
Your application goes through a rigorous screening process before you even enter an office.

selectionSteps.title: Step 2: Skill Test & Personal Interview (The Core Evaluation)
bullets:
The Skill Test: You will likely be sat in front of a computer.
Expectations: They might ask you to format a table in Excel, draft a formal memo in Word, or organize a PowerPoint slide.
Pro Tip: Speed is good, but accuracy is king . A single typo in a government-associated document can be a major red flag.
The Personal Interview: This isn’t just about what you know; it’s about how you carry yourself.

Key Focus: They will test your “Professional Aptitude.” They want to see if you can handle the pressure of a New Delhi head office. Be prepared for situational questions like, “How would you handle two urgent tasks from two different seniors at the same time?”
This is the most critical phase. Because this is an “Office Assistant” role, your practical utility is more important than your theoretical knowledge.
The Skill Test: You will likely be sat in front of a computer.
Expectations: They might ask you to format a table in Excel, draft a formal memo in Word, or organize a PowerPoint slide.

selectionSteps.title: Step 3: Document Verification (The Integrity Check)
bullets: What to bring: You must have your original degree, 10th and 12th certificates, and most importantly, your Experience Letters .
Warning: Ensure your experience letters are on the official letterhead of your previous employer with a contactable reference. Any discrepancy here can lead to immediate disqualification.
If you pass the interview, you’ll be called to show your original “credentials.”

selectionSteps.title: Step 4: Medical Examination (The Final Clearance)
bullets: What to expect: Basic tests like vision, blood pressure, and general physical health. This is usually the least stressful part, provided you are generally healthy and fit for desk work.
The final step is a routine medical check-up to ensure you are fit for the physical demands of an office environment.

selectionSteps.title: Final Success Strategy for You
bullets: Research the NBA: Spend 30 minutes on the NBA India website. Understanding that they deal with the “Accreditation of Higher Education” will make your interview answers sound much more informed and professional.
Dress the Part: For the interview/skill test, wear formal business attire. In a prestigious organization like the NBA, first impressions are lasting.
Patience is Key: Recruitment through BECIL can sometimes take a few weeks between stages. Keep a close eye on your email (including the spam folder) for the “Notify Later” updates.

selectionSteps.title: How to Apply for Palwal Court Recruitment 2026 (Offline)
bullets: Download the application form/proforma from the official website: palwal.dcourts.gov.i n (under Recruitments section).
Fill the application on plain paper (separate form for each post – one form for both will be rejected).
Affix a recent passport-size photograph on top.
Include details: Name, Father’s/Husband’s Name, DOB, Gender, Qualification, Category, Mobile No., Address, Experience (if any).
Attach self-attested copies of: Educational certificates, Category certificate (if reserved), ID proof, etc.
Send the completed application via Speed Post / Registered Post or by hand to: The District and Sessions Judge, Court Complex, Village Kuslipur, Palwal, Haryana – 121102.
Ensure it reaches by 10 March 2026 (5:00 PM) . No applications via email or after due date.
Important: Visit the website regularly for exam date, venue, and updates.

- The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has released the Admit Card for the Multi-Tasking (Non-Technical) Staff and Havaldar (CBIC & CBN) Examination 2025 (commonly referred to as SSC MTS 2026 cycle in many updates). This recruitment is for filling up thousands of entry-level government jobs suitable for 10th pass candidates across various central government departments, ministries, and organizations like Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) and Central Board of Narcotics (CBN).
The Computer-Based Test (CBT) is scheduled to begin from 04 February 2026 onwards in multiple shifts and dates. Admit cards are being released region-wise approximately 2-4 days before the candidate’s specific exam date. As of now (early February 2026), admit cards for initial dates (04-06 Feb and subsequent) are available on the official SSC portal. Candidates must download their e-Admit Card immediately, verify details, and prepare for the exam. Without a valid printed admit card and original photo ID proof, entry to the exam center will not be permitted.
This is one of the most popular SSC recruitments for 10th pass youth, offering stable government jobs with good pay scale, allowances, job security, and promotion opportunities.

metadata.shortInformation:
- Are you a fresh graduate looking to kickstart your career in the banking sector? The Bank of Baroda (BOB) Apprentice Recruitment 2025 is your golden opportunity! Bank of Baroda, one of India’s leading public sector banks, has officially released the notification for 2700 Apprentice vacancies under the Apprentices Act, 1961. This recruitment drive aims to engage young talent for practical training and hands-on experience in banking operations, customer service, and more. The online application process has just begun on November 11, 2025, and will close on December 1, 2025. If you meet the eligibility criteria, don’t miss this chance to join a prestigious organization like BOB.
In this comprehensive guide, we’ll cover everything you need to know about the BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2025 – from eligibility, application fees, selection process, to important dates and direct links. Whether you’re preparing for your first job or seeking skill enhancement, this apprenticeship program offers a stipend, exposure to real-world banking, and a stepping stone to future employment. Read on for detailed insights!
Overview of Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2025
Bank of Baroda is renowned for its employee-friendly policies and commitment to youth development. This year’s apprentice recruitment is designed to fill 2700 posts across various regions in India, focusing on graduates who can contribute fresh ideas to the banking ecosystem. The program duration is typically one year, during which apprentices will receive training in core banking functions, digital tools, and compliance standards.

metadata.shortInformation:
- The Rajasthan Staff Selection Board (RSSB), formerly known as RSMSSB, has officially announced the REET Mains Primary Teacher Recruitment 2025 notification for Level-1 Primary Teacher posts. This recruitment drive offers a significant opportunity for candidates who aspire to become government school teachers in Rajasthan. A total of 5,636 vacancies have been announced for Primary School Teachers (Class 1 to Class 5) under General Education and Sanskrit Education departments.
Candidates who have successfully qualified REET Level-1 examination are eligible to participate in this recruitment process. The recruitment is part of the larger REET Mains Teacher Recruitment 2025 campaign, which includes vacancies for both Primary and Upper Primary levels.
Teaching jobs in Rajasthan are among the most sought-after government careers because they provide job security, attractive salary packages, career growth opportunities, and the chance to contribute to the education system. Therefore, interested candidates should carefully understand the eligibility conditions, application process, examination pattern, syllabus, and selection procedure before applying online.
